2605.08406.title: Effective Explanations Support Planning Under Uncertainty
2605.08406.paper_summary: Explaining how to get from A to B can be challenging. It requires mentally simulating what the listener will do based on what they are told. To capture this process, we propose a computational model that converts utterances into action plans: a large language model translates an explanation into program-like guidance (a policy prior and value map), and a planning agent executes it under partial observability. We score explanations by the efficiency and reliability of the resulting paths, penalizing replanning. Across four preregistered experiments, we collect a corpus of 1,200 explanations over 24 maps, elicit helpfulness judgments, measure baseline navigation, and test behavior with explanations of differing quality. Higher-scored explanations are judged more helpful and improve navigation: participants with explanations outperform those without, and high-scoring explanations help more than low-scoring ones. Together, these results show procedural explanation as utility-guided communication shaped by how language can be grounded into action under uncertainty.
2605.08406.paper_summary_zh: 解釋如何從 A 到 B 可能是具有挑戰性的。這需要根據所告知的內容，心理上模擬聽者將會做什麼。為了捕捉這一過程，我們提出了一個計算模型，將話語轉換為行動計劃：一個大型語言模型將解釋轉換為類似程序的指導（政策先驗和價值地圖），而規劃代理在部分可觀察性下執行它。我們通過結果路徑的效率和可靠性來評分解釋，並對重新規劃進行懲罰。在四個預註冊的實驗中，我們收集了 1,200 條解釋，涵蓋 24 張地圖，提取有用性評價，測量基線導航，並測試不同質量解釋下的行為。得分較高的解釋被認為更有幫助，並改善導航：有解釋的參與者表現優於沒有解釋的參與者，而高得分的解釋比低得分的解釋更有幫助。綜合這些結果顯示，程序性解釋作為實用性導向的交流，受到語言如何在不確定性下被基於行動的影響。
2605.08406.author: Hanqi Zhou et.al.
2605.08406.authors: Hanqi Zhou, Britt Besch, Charley M. Wu, Tobias Gerstenberg
